name: Seljuk Empire
note: Oghuz Turkic rulers adopted Persian as court language; patronized Persian scholars — Omar Khayyam, al-Ghazali, Nizam al-Mulk all flourished under Seljuk rule

figures.name: Tughril
figures.significance: Entered Baghdad (1055) as Abbasid protector; received the title Sultan

figures.name: Alp Arslan
figures.significance: Defeated Byzantium at Manzikert (1071) — opened Anatolia to Turkic settlement

figures.name: Nizam al-Mulk
figures.significance: Grand vizier; his Siyasatnama is the masterwork of Persian administrative theory

figures.name: Omar Khayyam
figures.significance: Mathematician, astronomer, and poet (Rubaiyat); pinnacle of Seljuk Persian intellectual culture

name: Sultanate of Rum
note: Rumi composed his poetry in Persian at this court in Konya

figures.name: Jalal ad-Din Rumi
figures.significance: Composed the Masnavi in Persian at the Konya court; founder of the Mevlevi order; most widely read poet in the world

name: Ottoman Empire
note: Claimed the Caliphate title after conquering Mamluk Egypt (1517). Unlike earlier Turkic states, developed a distinct Turkic-Islamic identity rather than Persianizing. Ruled Orthodox Christians, Catholics, Jews, and Arabs as separate millets (religious communities) — religion defined the subject's legal identity, not ethnicity

policies.name: Millet System
policies.description: Non-Muslim communities (Orthodox Christians, Catholics, Jews) governed by their own religious laws in personal matters. Religion defined the legal identity of subjects, not ethnicity.

policies.name: Tanzimat Reforms
policies.description: Modernizing reforms granting equal rights to non-Muslim subjects, weakening the millet system. Policy shift — Ottoman ideology remained Sunni Caliphate.

figures.name: Osman I
figures.significance: The dynasty is named after him (Osmanli)

figures.name: Mehmed II (Fatih)
figures.significance: Conquered Constantinople (1453); ended the Byzantine Empire; claimed Kayser-i Rum

figures.name: Suleiman the Magnificent
figures.significance: Zenith of the Ottoman Empire; also the Lawgiver who reformed the legal system

figures.name: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk
figures.significance: Ended the Sultanate (1922) and Caliphate (1924); founded the Republic of Turkey
